questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.text: It is a government agency that registers and manages all domain names globally
It is a company that develops and maintains the Chrome and Firefox web browsers
It is a university consortium that funds research into network security protocols
It is the main international standards organization for the World Wide Web

questions.explanationMd: W3C develops web standards and specifications so that the Web remains interoperable across platforms and browsers.

questions.questionMd: In the context of the WWW, what is hypertext?

options.text: A protocol for securely transferring files between computers
A database format used to store web server logs
A programming language used to style web page layouts
A text document that contains links (hyper-links) to other text documents

questions.explanationMd: Hypertext is text containing hyperlinks that let users navigate to related documents or sections.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.text: SGML is a stylesheet language used to define the visual presentation of XML documents
SGML is a scripting language that adds interactivity to static HTML pages
SGML is a standard for specifying markup languages and was used by Tim Berners-Lee to create the HTML specification
SGML is a transport protocol that HTTP was built upon

questions.explanationMd: HTML was originally defined as an SGML application; SGML itself is a meta-language for defining markup languages.

questions.questionMd: Which keyword is used to create a new instance of an ES6 class?

options.text: init
create
instantiate
new

questions.explanationMd: JavaScript uses the `new` keyword to create an instance from a class or constructor function.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet respectively?

options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranet), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet
LANs use wireless connections only, while WANs use wired connections only
LANs are always public networks, while WANs are always private networks
LANs operate at the application layer, while WANs operate at the physical layer

questions.explanationMd: A LAN typically represents a local private network, while WAN connections link many networks over large geographic areas, enabling the broader Internet.

questions.questionMd: Compared to HTTP/1.1, what is a key advantage of HTTP/2.0?

options.text: HTTP/2.0 encrypts all data by default using SSL certificates
HTTP/2.0 eliminates the need for DNS lookups before connecting to a server
HTTP/2.0 keeps the connection open and allows multiple resources to be fetched simultaneously
HTTP/2.0 only supports text based content and not binary data

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 supports multiplexing, allowing multiple streams over a single connection, which improves loading efficiency.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was originally developed in 1969 by which organization to connect computers at research facilities and universities?

options.text: European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)
World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)
Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)
Transmission Control Protocol Institute (TCPI)

questions.explanationMd: ARPANET, funded by DARPA, is commonly cited as the origin of the modern Internet.

questions.questionMd: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1991 as a way to link research papers using hyperlinks?

options.text: Steve Jobs
Bill Gates
Tim Berners-Lee
Vint Cerf

questions.explanationMd: Tim Berners-Lee created the World Wide Web while working at CERN.

questions.questionMd: Which set of programming languages is associated with front-end web development?

options.text: Python, Java, and Ruby
SQL, XML, and SGML
HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
Node.js, PHP, and Go

questions.explanationMd: The traditional front-end stack is HTML for structure, CSS for styling, and JavaScript for behavior.

questions.questionMd: On which TCP port does an HTTP server listen by default?
questions.explanationMd: Port 80 is the standard default port for HTTP traffic.

questions.questionMd: In JavaScript, what is a closure?

options.text: A special syntax for defining private class fields in ES6
A function declared inside another function that retains access to the outer function's variables
A technique for combining multiple event listeners into a single handler
A method that prevents other code from modifying an object's properties

questions.explanationMd: A closure occurs when a function remembers variables from its lexical scope even after the outer function has returned.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `http://www.example.com:8080`, what does the value `8080` represent?

options.text: The TCP port on which the HTTP server is listening
The subnet mask of the server's IP address
The version number of the HTTP protocol being used
The number of packets sent in the HTTP request

questions.explanationMd: The number after the colon in a URL indicates the server port.

questions.questionMd: How are public fields defined on a class instance in ES6?

options.text: By assigning `this.fieldName = value` inside the constructor or a method
By using the `var` keyword at the top of the class body
By declaring them with the `public` keyword before the constructor
By listing them as parameters in the class declaration line

questions.explanationMd: A common ES6 class pattern is to assign instance properties through `this` in the constructor or methods.

questions.questionMd: What is the concept of currying in the context of functional JavaScript?

options.text: Binding an event listener to multiple HTML elements at the same time
Converting a synchronous function into an asynchronous Promise-based function
Defining a class method that can accept a variable number of parameters
Constructing a new function that is partially pre-filled with arguments from an outer function

questions.explanationMd: Currying transforms a function into a sequence of functions that capture arguments step by step.

questions.questionMd: What happens to the variables declared in an outer function when a closure references them, even after the outer function has returned?

options.text: They are kept alive because the closure holds a reference to them
They are immediately garbage-collected since the outer function has finished
They are reset to their default values on the next function call
They become global variables accessible from anywhere in the program

questions.explanationMd: Referenced outer variables stay alive as long as a closure can still access them.

questions.questionMd: What is the concept of currying in the context of functional JavaScript?

options.text: Converting a synchronous function into an asynchronous Promise-based function
Defining a class method that can accept a variable number of parameters
Constructing a new function that is partially pre-filled with arguments from an outer function
Binding an event listener to multiple HTML elements at the same time

questions.explanationMd: Currying creates a function that captures some arguments and returns another function for the remaining ones.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary role of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP)?

options.text: To assign unique IP addresses to devices on the Internet
To convert domain names into numerical IP addresses
To encrypt data transmitted between two computers
To transport hypertext documents from web servers to client computers

questions.explanationMd: HTTP is the protocol used to transfer web resources such as HTML from servers to clients.

questions.questionMd: On which TCP port does an HTTP server listen by default?
questions.explanationMd: Standard HTTP traffic uses TCP port 80 unless configured otherwise.

questions.questionMd: Which keyword is used to create a new instance of an ES6 class?

options.text: new
create
instantiate
init

questions.explanationMd: The `new` keyword instantiates a class.

questions.questionMd: When defining methods inside an ES6 class body, which keyword must you omit?

options.text: class
const
return
function

questions.explanationMd: Class methods are declared without the `function` keyword inside a class body.

questions.questionMd: Compared to HTTP/1.1, what is a key advantage of HTTP/2.0?

options.text: HTTP/2.0 eliminates the need for DNS lookups before connecting to a server
HTTP/2.0 keeps the connection open and allows multiple resources to be fetched simultaneously
HTTP/2.0 only supports text-based content and not binary data
HTTP/2.0 encrypts all data by default using SSL certificates

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 multiplexing allows several resources to be transferred concurrently over one connection.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is an important limitation of the CustomEvent API in JavaScript?

options.text: CustomEvents can only be created inside ES6 class constructors
CustomEvents can only be dispatched and listened to on HTML elements, not on arbitrary class instances
CustomEvents cannot carry any additional data in their event object
CustomEvents are not supported in any browser that uses the V8 engine

questions.explanationMd: The lecture frames CustomEvent as DOM-event oriented rather than a general arbitrary-instance event system.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a characteristic of first-class functions in JavaScript?

options.text: Functions cannot be stored in variables or object properties
Functions can be passed as parameters to other functions
Functions must always be declared before the class definition
Functions require the `function` keyword and cannot use arrow syntax

questions.explanationMd: Because functions are first-class values, they can be stored, passed around, and returned.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) in Internet communication?

options.text: To ensure the integrity of network communication
To define the visual layout of web pages
To translate domain names into IP addresses
To assign unique MAC addresses to network devices

questions.explanationMd: TCP is responsible for reliable, ordered delivery of data between hosts.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.text: SGML is a transport protocol that HTTP was built upon
SGML is a standard for specifying markup languages and was used by Tim Berners-Lee to create the HTML specification
SGML is a scripting language that adds interactivity to static HTML pages
SGML is a stylesheet language used to define the visual presentation of XML documents

questions.explanationMd: HTML was designed as an SGML application, so SGML served as the meta-language foundation.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is the JavaScript coding convention for indicating that a method is intended to be private?

options.text: Wrapping the method body inside a closure immediately
Declaring the method with `const` instead of inside the class body
Prefixing the method name with an underscore, e.g. `_methodName()`
Using the `private` keyword before the method definition

questions.explanationMd: A leading underscore is a common convention to signal intended privacy, even though it is not enforced by classic syntax.

questions.questionMd: When using the concise arrow function syntax, under what conditions can you omit the curly braces and the `return` keyword?

options.text: When the function takes more than two parameters
When the function body contains only a single return statement
When the function is assigned to a variable declared with `var`
When the function is defined inside a class constructor

questions.explanationMd: Arrow functions allow an implicit return only for a single expression body.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `http://www.example.com:8080`, what does the value `8080` represent?

options.text: The version number of the HTTP protocol being used
The number of packets sent in the HTTP request
The TCP port on which the HTTP server is listening
The subnet mask of the server's IP address

questions.explanationMd: The port in the URL tells the client which service port on the server to connect to.

questions.questionMd: What problem does calling `this.methodName = this.methodName.bind(this)` in a constructor solve?

options.text: It copies the method's code into the HTML element for faster execution
It ensures `this` always refers to the class instance when the method is used as an event handler
It registers the method as a global function accessible from any scope
It prevents the method from being called more than once per event

questions.explanationMd: Binding fixes the `this` value so callbacks still refer to the class instance.

questions.questionMd: ES6 classes were introduced in which version of the ECMAScript standard?

options.text: ECMAScript 3 (ES1999)
ECMAScript 5 (ES2009)
ECMAScript 8 (ES2017)
ECMAScript 6 (ES2015)

questions.explanationMd: Class syntax was introduced with ES2015, commonly called ES6.
